title: Focus on Africa: Transitions and Relationships
summary: This spring, the Belfer Center organized a new event series called Africa in Focus to examine the many factors driving political transitions and geostrategic relations in Africa. This workshop series aims to kickstart an initiative that recruits more African policymakers and experts to discuss African politics and affairs with the Harvard Kennedy School community.
body: Featured in the Spring 2022 Newsletter » This Spring, the Belfer Center launched a new event series called Africa in Focus to examine the many factors driving political transitions and geostrategic relations in Africa. This workshop series, directed by Natalie Colbert , Executive Director of the Belfer Center, and three graduating students, Hadja Diallo , Kadija Diallo , and Nicholas Sung , aims to center African voices and perspectives in policy discussions at Harvard Kennedy School (HKS). Ultimately, this team hopes to kickstart an initiative that intentionally recruits more African policymakers and experts to discuss African politics and affairs with students, staff, fellows, and faculty. The first event of the series, moderated by HKS graduate and development professional Awab Elmesbah , explored the role that civil society and populist movements have played in shaping demands for political transition in Sudan and Ethiopia, both past and present. The second event , moderated by Zoe Marks , Lecturer in Public Policy at HKS and Belfer Center Board Member, delved into political and security drivers of the recent coup d’états, with a focus on the complex nature and history of civil-military relations in West Africa. The final event , moderated by Colbert, focused on the nexus of political and economic interests between African governments, China, and the United States, as well as how African governments craft their foreign policies with an eye toward maximizing choice in partnerships with external actors. The latest from Belfer See more Gallery 1 of 3 2 of 3 3 of 3 The impetus for this initiative comes in part from the students’ observations that there are few events, panels, or classes on African politics at the Kennedy School. Sung noted that discussions on African affairs at HKS only occasionally feature panelists or speakers from the continent. “In my experience, American and European policymakers are usually spotlighted as speakers for panels on African affairs. African perspectives are in the room, but they are in the audience and only get to speak during the ‘Q&A’ portion of events. So, it is exciting that the Belfer Center is starting the Africa in Focus series to center African voices in these policy discussions.” Hadja Diallo echoed a similar sentiment: “As it was intended, the series helped start addressing the absence of discussion and integration of African politics and policies at HKS. The amount of support that the series has received from the Harvard community and the Belfer Center – from the very first conversation I had with Natalie Colbert about creating Africa-focused programming – reaffirmed the need for and importance of Africa in Focus.” Additional programming on Africa topics is still in the initial stages of development, but the team is already planning for the next semester. “We are exploring ways to advance conversations around topics including the security, economic, and political impacts of climate change in Africa, as well as current trends and innovators in Africa’s tech ecosystem,” said Colbert. The Belfer Center is excited to continue these efforts in the next academic year. As Kadija Diallo said, “Africa has one of the largest and youngest populations in the world, so the future of the world is going to be the future of Africa. So, it is important that Harvard creates space to have conversations about the continent with people from the continent.” She added, “it’s encouraging to see the Belfer Center lead in creating spaces for these conversations.” Recommended citation “Focus on Africa: Transitions and Relationships ” Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The Belfer Center at Harvard Kennedy School launched the 'Africa in Focus' event series in Spring 2022 to examine political transitions and geostrategic relations in Africa, aiming to center African voices in policy discussions. The series, directed by Natalie Colbert and three students, addressed the observed lack of African-focused programming at HKS. The first event explored civil society and populist movements in Sudan and Ethiopia. The second analyzed drivers of coups and civil-military relations in West Africa. The final event focused on the foreign policy nexus between African governments, China, and the U.S., and how African states maximize choice in external partnerships. The initiative received strong support, highlighting the need for more African perspectives. Future programming plans include discussions on climate change impacts and Africa's tech ecosystem.
result.main_content_zh: 哈佛大学肯尼迪学院贝尔弗中心于2022年春季启动了“聚焦非洲”系列活动，旨在探讨非洲的政治转型与地缘战略关系，并将非洲的声音置于政策讨论的核心。该系列由纳塔莉·科尔伯特与三名学生主导，旨在应对学院内非洲议题讨论不足的现状。首场活动探讨了苏丹和埃塞俄比亚的民间社会与民粹主义运动。第二场活动深入分析了西非政变的驱动因素及军民关系的复杂性。最后一场活动聚焦于非洲政府与中国、美国之间的政治经济关系，以及非洲国家如何制定外交政策以最大化其在外部伙伴关系中的选择权。该倡议获得了广泛支持，证实了引入更多非洲视角的必要性。团队正计划未来的活动，议题包括气候变化对非洲安全、经济和政治的影响，以及非洲科技生态系统的当前趋势与创新。
